title: Recurrent Sore Throat - Chronic Tonsillitis
chief_complaint: 12-year-old with recurrent tonsillitis: 7 episodes in past year with fever, sore throat, and cervical lymphadenopathy. Enlarged tonsils causing snoring and difficulty swallowing. Afebrile today.

physical_exam.throat: Enlarged tonsils (3+), cryptic, no exudate currently, uvula midline

patient_responses.infections: I get strep throat almost every month
patient_responses.school: I've missed 20 days of school this year
patient_responses.snoring: My sister says I snore loudly

feedback.correct_message: Well assessed! She meets criteria for tonsillectomy.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is chronic recurrent tonsillitis meeting Paradise criteria for surgery.
feedback.attending_note: Tonsillectomy: Meets Paradise criteria (>7 episodes/year). Advise parents about post-op pain (7-10 days), risk of hemorrhage (2-5%), and importance of hydration. Avoid NSAIDs post-op (aspirin/ibuprofen) - use acetaminophen.
